clinical_history: Elderly woman with an irregular pulse. What is the rhythm? What other abnormalities are present.
diagnosis: The ECG shows baseline artifact (simulating a premature ventricular complex at the end of the recording). The rhythm is atrial fibrillation with a relatively rapid ventricular response at rest (mean about 100/min; range about 85 to 125/min). A complete right bundle branch block (RBBB) is present. The QRS voltage is relatively low diffusely. Non-specific ST-T changes are present. Note that the fibrillatory waves are relatively fine (low amplitude and high frequency) and may be missed entirely. At times higher amplitude f waves simulate sinus P waves. The ventricular rate may look regular at first glance, but if you measure the R-R intervals carefully, beat-by-beat, they are always changing. AF is one of the most commonly over- and under-diagnosed major arrhythmias.
